mandarin.breakdown: Polite hello for elders or strangers.
Slightly more formal than 谢谢.
Casual response to thanks or apology.
Zero, used in phones and scores.
Used before measure words, not 二.

mandarin.pinyin: Nǐ de shēngrì shì nǎ tiān?
Nǐ de shēngrì shì nǎ tiān?

scenarios.title: Ordering Street Food
scenarios.description: You are exploring a bustling night market and smell delicious dumplings.

phrasebook.hanzi: 请问，有什么馅儿的饺子？
phrasebook.pinyin: Qǐngwèn, yǒu shénme xiànr de jiǎozi?
phrasebook.english: Excuse me, what fillings do the dumplings have?

phrasebook.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng yào yí fèn jiǎozi.
phrasebook.english: I'd like one portion of dumplings.
I want pork and cabbage filling.
How much is it in total?
Can it be a little cheaper?

scenarios.description: You have just arrived at your hotel after a long flight.

scenarios.description: You are in a local gift shop looking for a present.

scenarios.description: You are lost in the city center.
